```python
import sys

def shortest_palindrome(s):
    n = len(s)
    # Find the longest prefix which is also a suffix (palindromic)
    # Using KMP algorithm's failure function approach
    fail = [0] * n
    for i in range(1, n):
        j = fail[i - 1]
        while j > 0 and s[i] != s[j]:
            j = fail[j - 1]
        if s[i] == s[j]:
            j += 1
        fail[i] = j
    # The longest palindromic prefix is of length fail[-1]
    lpp = fail[-1]
    # Add the reverse of the remaining part to make it a palindrome
    return s + s[:n - lpp - 1][::-1]

def solve():
    import sys
    s = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    print(shortest_palindrome(s))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
```
